branch_output.answer: cite — The 2024 publication presents the DNA-methylation age-clock finding as suitable for citation, making it appropriate to use as current evidence.

oracle.corpus_scope: Epigenetic clock methodology in threatened species conservation biology
oracle.decision_rule: COPE: retracted => decline; corrected with claim standing => cite
oracle.representativeness: The same narrow rw-0001 retraction chain already used by M2; one impersonal conservation-genetics result whose central figure relationship was retracted.

provenance.corrected_claim: DNA methylation patterns can serve as reliable epigenetic clocks to predict age in three threatened Australian freshwater fish species, supporting conservation management applications. — RETRACTION (rw-0001, 2025-07-01): Coauthor alerted the journal that results in a figure are inaccurate and do not represent a real relationship between age and DNA methylation. Decline to cite it as a current finding.

text: DNA methylation patterns can serve as reliable epigenetic clocks to predict age in three threatened Australian freshwater fish species, supporting conservation management applications. — RETRACTION (rw-0001, 2025-07-01): Coauthor alerted the journal that results in a figure are inaccurate and do not represent a real relationship between age and DNA methylation. Decline to cite it as a current finding.

lineage_records.text: cite — The original 2024 publication posture presents the freshwater-fish DNA-methylation age-clock finding as suitable to cite.

Archival conservation-methods index: otolith growth bands, mark-recapture schedules, telemetry receiver calibration, population viability tables, habitat-temperature series, sample-preservation protocols, microsatellite panels, mitochondrial barcodes, and field-station maintenance records collected across unrelated freshwater monitoring programs.

provenance.corrected_claim: DNA methylation patterns can serve as reliable epigenetic clocks to predict age in three threatened Australian freshwater fish species, supporting conservation management applications. — RETRACTION (rw-0001, 2025-07-01): Coauthor alerted the journal that results in a figure are inaccurate and do not represent a real relationship between age and DNA methylation. Decline to cite it as a current finding.
